question:
Will Stokes Twins' next video get between 18–20 million views on week 1?

Description: This market will resolve according to the number of views the next YouTube video posted by Stokes Twins gets in the first 7 days after being posted.

If Stokes Twins does not post a YouTube video by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this is Stokes Twins' YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@StokesTwins), specifically the 'views' counter for the described video.

Note: This market refers to Stokes Twins' next video posted. Shorts, previews, or other videos released other than the referenced video will not be considered.

evidences.title: Stokes Twins - Wikipedia
evidences.content: Alan and Alex Chen Stokes (born November 23, 1996), commonly known as the Stokes Twins, are Americans twins and influencers known for their YouTube and TikTok accounts. They began making videos separately and then combined their following into a shared twins account. In Forbes’ 2025 Top Creators list the Stokes Twins ranked #19, with the magazine estimating their 12-month earnings at roughly $20 million. evidences.title: 2025 YouTube Algorithm: A Detailed Guide for Creators
evidences.content: ## Top Ranking Factors for YouTube Algorithm in 2025 YouTube algorithms in 2025 decide which videos appear in search, recommendations, and the homepage. The algorithm holistically analyzes signals to maximize engagement. Here are five key factors to elevate your channel, with practical YouTube algorithm tips. ### Click-Through Rate (CTR) The YouTube algorithm assesses how often viewers click a video after seeing it. A 5–7% CTR indicates strong thumbnails and titles. For example, “How to Bake Delicious Bread at Home” with an appetizing loaf thumbnail may hit 6% CTR. Avoid clickbait—content must deliver on promises, or retention drops. ### Watch Time and Audience Retention [...] ### Key Metrics Driving the Algorithm The YouTube algorithm relies on metrics reflecting engagement and retention: watch time, click-through rate (CTR), audience retention, and feedback (likes, comments, shares, and “Not Interested” tags). These help YouTube identify videos that keep users on the platform, warranting promotion in recommendations. Core Metrics of YouTube’s Algorithm For YouTube Shorts, replay count is key, unlike CTR for long-form videos. User Signals and Their Impact [...] ### Metadata Opt...

evidences.title: How the YouTube algorithm works in 2025 - Hootsuite Blog
evidences.content: : How often users click on your video when they see it in search results or recommendations. Watch time & retention: The total minutes watched and the average percentage of the video viewed. Likes, comments, and shares: These indicate strong audience interest and engagement. Negative feedback: If users frequently click “Not Interested” or dislike your video, YouTube may suppress recommendations. How to boost viewer engagement signals: Improve thumbnails and titles to boost CTR, and focus on delivering value quickly to improve retention. ### 2. Viewer satisfaction YouTube prioritizes videos that provide a positive viewing experience, not just those that hold attention the longest. [...] YouTube pulls videos that each viewer is most likely to watch and enjoy, using factors like watch time, click-through rate (CTR), satisfaction surveys, and viewing habits across different devices and times of day. The algorithm’s goal is to show users the most relevant and valuable content in order to keep them engaged and on the platform. YouTube’s recommendation algorithm drives 70% of what people watch on the platform, so it’s no surprise that marketers, influencers, and creators are obsessed w...

evidences.title: YouTubers the Stokes Twins Sign Netflix Deal
evidences.content: The brothers have signed a deal with the streamer that will bring an archive of their YouTube content to Netflix starting July 18. The Stokes are also developing a long-form show for Netflix, slated to premiere in 2027; details on that project are being kept under wraps for now. The content deal with Netflix is non-exclusive; the Stokes Twins have more than 140 million subscribers on YouTube, and their videos will remain there. (The brothers also have millions of followers on TikTok, Instagram and other social platforms.) ### Related Stories TF1 Home Row [Business ### TF1 Says Its Netflix Deal Has Already Paid Off]( Skywalker Hughes, Luke Bracey, Alice Halsey of 'Little House on the Prairie' [TV [...] an image, when javascript is unavailable ### The Definitive Voice of Entertainment News Subscribe for full access to The Hollywood Reporter ## site categories ### Warner Bros.’ The Ranch Lands LA28 Broadcast Center In Shake Up for Summer Games ### The Definitive Voice of Entertainment News Subscribe for full access to The Hollywood Reporter # YouTube Stars the Stokes Twins Sign Netflix Deal (Exclusive) An archive of the brothers' content will debut on the streamer July 18, and they...
